steps.title: Quick question first…
steps.question: If you increase the resistance in a circuit (keeping voltage the same), what happens to the current?
steps.misconceptionFeedback: ⚠️ Common misconception! Resistance opposes the flow of current. If you increase resistance while keeping the voltage the same, the current must decrease — there is more opposition to the flow of charge. Voltage does not automatically increase.
steps.correctFeedback: ✓ Correct! Increasing resistance reduces current (for a fixed voltage). Resistance is like a narrow section of pipe — it restricts the flow of charge.
steps.bestSource: BEST resource: AQA Physics specification 4.2.1 — Resistance

steps.title: Resistance
steps.bodyHTML: Resistance is the opposition to the flow of current. It is measured in ohms (Ω). A higher resistance means less current flows (for a given voltage). Resistance depends on the length, cross-sectional area, and material of a conductor. Longer wires have more resistance; thicker wires have less resistance.

relationships: Bigger V → bigger R (if I stays the same)
Bigger I → smaller R (if V stays the same)

commonErrors: Confusing resistance with resistivity (a material property)
Forgetting the unit is ohms (Ω), not volts or amps
Thinking resistance causes current — it opposes it

steps.title: See it in action
steps.instruction: Drag the sliders to change voltage and current. Watch how resistance changes.

aqa.questionText: A resistor has a potential difference of 6.0 V across it and a current of 0.25 A flowing through it. Calculate the resistance of the resistor.

steps.label: Apply R = V / I
steps.markSchemeNote: 1 mark — correct equation; 1 mark — substitution; 1 mark — correct answer + unit

edexcel.questionText: A component has a resistance of 15 Ω and a current of 0.80 A flowing through it. Calculate the potential difference across the component.

steps.label: Substitute and calculate
steps.markSchemeNote: 1 mark — substitution; 1 mark — correct answer + unit

ocr-gateway.questionText: A bulb has a p.d. of 3.0 V and draws a current of 0.50 A. Calculate its resistance.

steps.label: Apply R = V / I
steps.markSchemeNote: 1 mark — substitution; 1 mark — correct answer + unit

ocr-21c.questionText: A wire has a resistance of 10 Ω. A current of 2.0 A flows through it. Calculate the p.d. across the wire.

steps.label: Apply V = IR
steps.markSchemeNote: 1 mark — substitution; 1 mark — correct answer + unit

wjec.questionText: A lamp draws a current of 0.40 A when connected to a 12 V supply. Calculate the resistance.

steps.label: Apply R = V / I
steps.markSchemeNote: 1 mark — substitution; 1 mark — correct answer + unit

aqa.questionText: A component has a p.d. of 9.0 V across it. A current of 0.30 A flows through it. What is the resistance?
aqa.markSchemeAward: 1 mark equation · 1 mark substitution · 1 mark correct answer + unit
aqa.commonMistake: Option A is multiplying I × V instead of dividing V by I.

edexcel.questionText: A 50 Ω resistor has a current of 0.40 A. What is the p.d. across it?
edexcel.markSchemeAward: 1 mark equation · 1 mark correct answer + unit

ocr-gateway.questionText: A heater has a resistance of 40 Ω. The p.d. across it is 8.0 V. Calculate the current.
ocr-gateway.markSchemeAward: 1 mark rearrangement · 1 mark correct answer + unit

ocr-21c.questionText: A 20 Ω resistor carries a current of 1.5 A. What p.d. is across it?
ocr-21c.markSchemeAward: 1 mark equation · 1 mark correct answer + unit

wjec.questionText: A bulb has a p.d. of 6.0 V and draws 0.50 A. What is its resistance?
wjec.markSchemeAward: 1 mark equation · 1 mark correct answer + unit

equation.examTip: This is the most commonly used equation in electricity. Make sure you can rearrange it for all three variables.

boardNotes.aqa: On AQA formula sheet. Used extensively — practise all rearrangements.
boardNotes.edexcel: On Edexcel equation sheet. Units must be stated for full marks.
boardNotes.ocr-gateway: NOT on formula sheet — must be memorised for OCR Gateway. ⚠️
boardNotes.ocr-21c: NOT on formula sheet — must be memorised for OCR 21st Century. ⚠️
boardNotes.wjec: Provided in WJEC exam. Know all three rearrangements.
